transcript.clean_text: first day of racing proper on this year's tirano adriatico stage two one of back-to-back sprinting days starting off in kameori 219 kilometers destination soviet chile well the first of this race's categorized climbs would offer up the temptation to a breakaway of a king of the mountains jersey the big looping course towards the end would have played havoc have the wind blown as per predictions in the end it was benign as far as the wind was concerned but plenty of action along the way well five riders were tempted to go in the breakaway two from elo cometa a brace from bardiani as well joining them morengo from drone hopper once the flag had fallen they were busy and let go straight away the gap went up to around seven and a half minutes but it was a loose accord and in fact started to fracture on the days only climb three kilometers out they were starting to battle each other for a jersey as you'd expect the wide open planes then giving way to lumpy terrain which would eventually lead to the mountains between the two seas that define the race setting off out front david baies of iloca on the wheel of canaveral of badiani and it was by east that sped on to take the mountain's jersey meaning they just had to finish the day and would be guaranteed a place on the podium halo committee one of the invitee teams always going to be a feature and indeed so it proved today and it wasn't over for well baise them through he looked back to see where his teammate was francesco gavazzi was the man he was waiting for and ultimately gabazzi was to go and join him and a brace of illa comita riders out front but would they make it home 57 kilometers is a long old distance especially the terrain was starting to be a feature and even though it was non-categorized still four climbs remained on the deck while drone hoppers morengo almost had a technical of convenience and indeed the breakaway was slowly but surely wounded doing most of the work a lot of saddam of course working hard for caleb ewan ultimately but with two up the road there was still a bonus second up for grabs at the intermediate sprint rather unkindly placed at the top of a citadel pushing on out of the pack who else but teddy picacho a man who knows that every second counts and that last bonus second was to go his way that seemed to be an action point with mark solaire magretch's teammate setting off up the roads well in good company of anthony perez and he sped past the two riders from ela committer and indeed went fully committed to try and reach the line first well with the final climb to be dealt with a six kilometer descent leading to the flatlands to our approach would so there indeed go for it was he off script we didn't know he's been known to take matters into his own hands in the past but with six kilometers to go when we finally hit the flat that would be our approach a bunch sprint was a certainty after a compressed corner with 3.2 to go the teams were getting themselves in color order for a dash to the line a gentle left-hander would define the last 350 meters you had to be in a good position to take it well they were all starting to get themselves in colour order but in the end it was to fall to pieces all the hard work by group armour festival and indeed a lot of sadal came to north those who had been anonymous throughout the day were surely going to take a step forward so it proved to be peter sagan total energies had worked hard to put him in a decent position and he went for it that indeed encouraged others around him and tim merlier hit the line first well well out of position until the very last was olaf koi the fastest finisher of the group he got up for second place remarkably being down in 15th with 200 meters remaining a terrific win great work by albertson fenix making sure that tim malia was out of the wind for much of the day you have to hit the line first and that's precisely what he did close at hand kaden grows from team bike exchange and of course the aforementioned mr coy stage three to tourney is what we're looking forward to tim malia has already taken a stage with can he repeat the feat cory will try and stop him and so were some very big names who had an off day mark cavendish with a light coil he'll be back on form we hope likewise kristoff viviani caleb ewing but you've got to beat tim malia today nobody could overall pippo ghana leads the race by the same margin that he carried over from the time trial save for the second that teleport catcher gained on those above well with an 11 second advantage only lots can change intermediate sprints notwithstanding there is of course a dash for the line tomorrow as the terrain gets slightly more lumpy that could play into the hands of a breakaway turning our destination not so long with 171 kilometers the weather's fabulous and so is the basic you must join us
